title: BVC Coffee — Teacher Resources (PDF)
description: Full teacher guide: IAS + Common Core alignment, 4 classroom activities with handouts, project rubrics, knowledge-check answer key, differentiation strategies, bibliography.

title: The Women's Petition Against Coffee (1674)
description: Satirical London broadside. Useful for ELA rhetorical analysis and Social Studies as primary-source evidence of coffeehouse cultural impact.

title: The Men's Answer to the Women's Petition (1674)
description: Counter-broadside. Pairs with the Women's Petition for rhetorical-rebuttal analysis.

title: Royal Proclamation for the Suppression of Coffee Houses (1675)
description: Charles II's 11-day coffeehouse ban. Useful for Social Studies discussion of state power vs. public sphere.

title: Colonial Coffee Plantation Records (18th c.)
description: Primary-source business documents from Caribbean/Brazilian coffee plantations. Use for critical reading of economic rhetoric and dehumanizing labor language.

title: Bunn et al. (2015) — climate change profile of global coffee production (PLOS ONE)
description: The peer-reviewed paper behind the '50% of coffee land unsuitable by 2050' projection. Open-access.
